title: A Metamorphic Testing Approach to Diagnosing Memorization in LLM-Based Program Repair
data:
M De Koning, A Asgari, P Derakhshanfar, A Panichella- arXiv preprint arXiv, 2026
LLM-based automated program repair (APR) techniques have shown promising 
results in reducing debugging costs. However, prior results can be affected by data 
leakage: large language models (LLMs) may memorize bug fixes when evaluation 
benchmarks overlap with their pretraining data, leading to inflated performance 
estimates. In this paper, we investigate whether we can better reveal data leakage by 
combining metamorphic testing (MT) with negative log-likelihood (NLL), which has

title: A Usability Study of Interpretable Aesthetic Evaluation as Decision Support in Full-Site AI Website Generators for Novice Users
data:
H Shi, C Chu- IEEE Access, 2026
Generative artificial intelligence systems increasingly enable novice users to create 
complex websites with minimal technical expertise; however, these users often 
struggle to evaluate the visual quality of AI-generated outputs and make confident 
refinement decisions. This study investigates whether an aesthetics-guided 
interaction design, integrating interpretable computational aesthetic evaluation into 
AI-powered website generation workflows can support users' evaluative judgment
